steps.title: Read the vertex from vertex form
steps.content: $f(x) = \frac{1}{8}(x-5)^2 + 3$ is in form $a(x-h)^2 + k$, so the vertex is at $(h, k) = (5, 3)$.

steps.title: Decide minimum or maximum
steps.content: Since $a = \frac{1}{8} \gt  0$, the parabola opens upward, so the vertex gives a minimum. The minimum height is $k = 3$ inches, occurring at $x = 5$ seconds (not at $x = 0$).

steps.title: Check each choice
steps.content:
• The toy train's minimum height was 3 inches above the floor.: Minimum height was 3 inches: matches $k = 3$ with $a \gt 0$.
  • The toy train's minimum height was 5 inches above the floor.: Minimum height was 5 inches: 5 is the $x$-coordinate (time), not the height.
  • The toy train's height was 3 inches above the floor when it started moving.: Height was 3 inches when it started: at $x = 0$, $f(0) = \frac{25}{8} + 3 = \frac{49}{8} \approx 6.13$, not 3.
  • The toy train's height was 5 inches above the floor when it started moving.: Height was 5 inches when it started: same issue, and 5 is the time of the minimum.
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
